videos.title: Norge Ski Club postpones annual ski jump tournament until February, due to lack of snow
videos.description:
A lack of snow and wintry weather has prompted the Norge Ski Club to postpone its 118th annual Winter Ski Jump Tournament in Fox River Grove.
*************************************************************
ABOUT CBS 2 NEWS CHICAGO: Honored as an Edward R. Murrow winner for overall excellence, CBS 2 News Chicago brings you breaking news, weather, compelling exclusive content and award-winning investigative reports from the CBS 2 Investigators. 
Subscribe to CBS 2 News Chicago on YouTube : http://www.youtube.com/user/cbschicago
CBS 2 NEWS 24/7 LIVESTREAM: https://www.cbsnews.com/chicago/live/
CBS 2 NEWS WEBSITE:  http://www.cbsnews.com/chicago
FACEBOOK: https://www.facebook.com/cbschicago
TWITTER: https://twitter.com/CBSchicago
GET THE CBS 2 NEWS APP: https://apps.cbslocal.com/chicago/

it's one of the most daring Sports

there's one big problem not enough snow

poles sticking up and you can turn them
adjust them up and down for us to make
snow we need roughly 20 degrees or
colder just because we use well water
and it's kind of warm temperatures are
not ideal and neither is the timing our
planning for this event in the winter is
literally six to eight months in advance
we start planting the club's annual
winter ski jump tournament was set for
this weekend the last 10 years we've
been typically getting eight to ten
thousand people over two days a crowd
expecting to see competition will have
to wait because of the one thing we just
had no choice none of us can control
Mother Nature the competition has never
been canceled and in its 118 year
history this is only the second
postponement it's a hard decision you
know believe me we would have loved what
pulled it off the tournament is pushed
back two weeks so we're just going
February 11th and 12th instead but
tradition will return when temperatures
and timing align as soon as we get down
to that temperature we'll fire these up
and start making it Noel Brennan CBS 2
News
the ski club says it's not offering
refunds but it will allow people to
transfer their tickets if they can't
make the rescheduled event

Fireside history with Nikki delventhal
so these ruins were occupied around 900
ad to like 1200 and they were ancestors
to the Hopi Zuni and peblo tribes that
is so cool um apparently they were
prehistoric farmers who grew corn beans
and squash and they stored their crops
in Pottery jars and greeneries that's
what those little rooms that we hiked to
were four that we h up into yeah those
were to store everything the little
square ones that were made from like the
soil mortar and the wooden beams mhm are
you channeling your inner Native
American when you talk about Native
American I know I've been to a lot of
Ceremonies and I would say I relate most
to Native American spirituality of
cultures for sure love that yeah I think
this is incredible probably why I'm so
fascinated by the history and of the the
ruins so what do the petroglyphs me that
we found oh like the spiral one okay so
spiral petroglyphs can have many
meanings including a representation of
the sun water migration or a calendar
which is really interesting I feel like
but apparently for the poblo ancestors
it mostly meant like the sky or the sun
um but maybe also like
solstices not Falling
Rocks didn't we see the same ones in uh
yeah it's a very common Petroglyph to
see um but also if you think about it in
the area we are where all the tribes
were they're very like very common
tribes but apparently a lot of people
will argue that the spiral represents
the Sun or the portal to the spirit
world so I think it has a very spiritual
meaning as well which I could definitely
see um perhaps it represented life
itself for Life Beyond life like
eternity or the Galaxy I this is so cool
so long story short find yourself in

videos.title: 2011-01-08 The Frosty Freestyle 1km Backup Race Course
videos.description: If there's not enough snow on the Frosty Freestyle course this coming Saturday, Jan 15, 2011, we'll use this alternative course on Maltby Lake in Huron Meadows Metropark. A beautiful prep job by Adam Haberkorn. Details at http://nordicskiracer.com/news.asp?NewsID=5308

videos.title: 2013 Frosty Freestyle Nordic Ski Race
videos.description: On January 12, 2013, 60 athletes defied Mother Nature and completed a 12k cross-country ski race on man-made snow at Huron Meadows Metropark in Brighton, Michigan. Just 3 days before the race, there was nearly 10" of beautiful, natural powder on the course, but rain showers and rising temperatures destroyed the regular trail. The crew at Huron Meadows used a stockpile of man-made snow to create a fun loop and the race went on as scheduled.
